evidence.title: README (segment 1)
evidence.excerpt: # MeshInfo Realtime web UI to run against a Meshtastic regional or private mesh network. [![Docker Image](https://github.com/MeshAddicts/meshinfo/actions/workflows/docker.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/MeshAddicts/meshinfo/actions/workflows/docker.yml) ![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/meshaddicts/meshinfo) ![GitHub commit activity](https://img.shields.io/github/commit-activity/t/meshaddicts/meshinfo) ## Overview MeshInfo is written in Python and connects to an MQTT server that is receiving Meshtastic messages for the purpose of visualizing and inspecting traffic. It (currently) uses a filesystem to persist content, such as node info and telemetry. There are plans to optionally support Postgres and SQLite3 as optional persistance storage methods. To make deployment to run an instance for your mesh easy, Docker support is included. We recommend using Docker Compose with a personalized version of the `docker-compose.yml` file to most easily deploy it, but any seasoned Docker user can also use the Docker image alone. If you use MeshInfo and have a publicly accessible instance, we'd like to know! Drop a note to kevin@airframes.io with details and we'll link…

evidence.title: v2.0.2
evidence.excerpt: > [!CAUTION] > **Breaking Change: PostgreSQL is now required.** > JSON/filesystem storage is deprecated and **will be removed in a future version**. All deployments must use PostgreSQL going forward. If you are currently using JSON storage, please follow the migration steps before upgrading. ## What's Changed * build(deps-dev): bump @types/node from 25.3.0 to 25.3.1 by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/MeshAddicts/meshinfo/pull/279 * Fix hex conversion by @SimmerV in https://github.com/MeshAddicts/meshinfo/pull/286 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/MeshAddicts/meshinfo/compare/v2.0.1...v2.0.2

evidence.title: v2.0.1
evidence.excerpt: > [!CAUTION] > **Breaking Change: PostgreSQL is now required.** > JSON/filesystem storage is deprecated as of this release and **will be removed in a future version**. All deployments must use PostgreSQL going forward. If you are currently using JSON storage, please follow the migration steps below before upgrading. ## Highlights **v2.0.1** is the first stable release of the v2 line, featuring a completely overhauled UI, mandatory PostgreSQL storage, config validation, structured logging, telemetry fixes, and native ARM64 CI builds. ### 🗄️ PostgreSQL Now Required PostgreSQL is now the only supported storage backend. JSON/filesystem storage is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Existing users **must** migrate their data to PostgreSQL before upgrading. #### New Deployments 1. Ensure `storage.postgres.enabled` is set to `true` in your `config.json` 2. Run `docker compose up -d` 3. Tables are created automatically on startup #### Upgrading from JSON Storage 1. Set `storage.postgres.enabled` to `true` in your `config.json` 2. Run `docker compose up -d` (tables are created automatically) 3. Run the migration script: ```bash docker exec -it meshinfo-meshinfo-1 python3 s…
